title: cornus gigantea
intro: Cornus gigantea, also known as the giant dogwood or cornelian cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to eastern Asia. Its bark and fruit have been used in traditional medicine for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The plant's extracts have been found to have various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-cancer, and cardiovascular protective effects. Cornus gigantea is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried bark, and has been used in herbal medicine to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
